description: Fix E2E Docker fixtures, verify each coding agent works end-to-end inside secure-exec (SDK → headless → TTY), verify agent tool use through the sandbox, and test real-world agentic workflows (multi-turn, npm install, dev servers).

userStories.title: Run pg-connect fixture against real Postgres container and fix until parity passes
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the pg-connect Docker fixture to connect to a real Postgres container through the sandbox's net bridge so we validate that the pg library works end-to-end inside secure-exec.
acceptanceCriteria: Start a real Postgres container (postgres:16-alpine) with testuser/testpass/testdb
Run the pg-connect fixture inside the sandbox via createTestNodeRuntime + proc.exec()
Fix any net bridge issues (net.Socket, net.connect, etc.) that prevent the pg library from connecting
Fixture creates table, inserts, queries, drops — all through the sandbox's network bridge to real Postgres
Update fixture.json expectation from 'fail' to 'pass' once parity is achieved
Host Node.js and sandbox produce identical normalized stdout/stderr/exit code
No mocking of Postgres — must use a real Docker container
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented net bridge with TCP socket support and crypto.subtle.deriveBits for SCRAM-SHA-256 auth. pg connects through the sandbox's net bridge to a real Postgres container.

Tests pass
userStories.notes: Fixed: iconv-lite lazy encoding load inside applySync contexts. Added sync resolution/loading bridges, JS-side resolution cache, name-based module caching, mysql_native_password auth, and eager iconv-lite init.

userStories.title: Rewrite pi-headless.test.ts to run Pi inside the secure-exec sandbox VM
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Pi headless tests to run Pi's JavaScript inside the secure-exec sandbox so the tests actually validate the sandbox's module loading, fs bridge, network bridge, and child_process bridge.
acceptanceCriteria: Test creates a NodeRuntime (using createTestNodeRuntime or equivalent pattern from test-suite tests)
Pi's entry point (dist/cli.js or createAgentSession from @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent) is loaded and executed inside the sandbox VM, NOT spawned as a host process
Mock LLM server stays on the host; sandbox code reaches it via ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL through the network bridge
All existing test scenarios preserved: boot, output, file read, file write, bash tool, JSON output
File read/write tests go through the sandbox's VFS/fs bridge, not host filesystem
No direct use of node:child_process.spawn from the test harness to run Pi
Tests skip with clear reason if Pi cannot load in the VM (e.g. ESM bridge gap)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Pi is pure JS with a createAgentSession() programmatic API. Its tools (bash, read, write) use child_process and fs which the sandbox bridges. Look at test-suite/node.test.ts for patterns on creating NodeRuntime and running code inside it. The current test explicitly admits 'Pi runs as a host process' in its header comment.

userStories.title: Rewrite pi-interactive.test.ts to use kernel.openShell() inside the sandbox
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Pi interactive tests to run Pi's TUI through the sandbox's PTY (kernel.openShell()) so the tests validate the sandbox's PTY line discipline, isTTY bridge, and signal delivery.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace PtyHarness (host 'script -qefc' spawn) with kernel.openShell() from the secure-exec kernel
Connect @xterm/headless to the sandbox's terminal output, not a host PTY
Pi's TUI renders inside the sandbox PTY, not a host PTY
All existing test scenarios preserved: TUI renders, input appears, prompt submission, Ctrl+C interrupt
No direct use of node:child_process.spawn from the test harness to run Pi
If kernel.openShell() or isTTY bridge is not ready, skip with clear reason referencing the specific blocker
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Rewritten: replaced PtyHarness (host script -qefc) with kernel.openShell() + TerminalHarness. Uses overlay VFS (InMemoryFileSystem for populateBin, host FS fallback for module resolution). Probes detect isTTY bridge gap (spec gap #5) and skip with clear reason. All 5 test scenarios preserved and ready for when isTTY is bridged.

userStories.title: Rewrite opencode-headless.test.ts to spawn opencode via sandbox child_process bridge
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the OpenCode headless tests to spawn the opencode binary from inside the secure-exec sandbox via the child_process.spawn bridge, so the tests validate stdio piping, env forwarding, signal delivery, and exit code propagation through the bridge.
acceptanceCriteria: Test creates a NodeRuntime and runs JS code inside the sandbox that calls child_process.spawn('opencode', ['run', ...])
The sandbox's child_process bridge spawns the real opencode binary on the host
Environment variables (ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) are forwarded through the bridge's env filtering
Stdout/stderr from the binary flow back through the bridge to sandbox code
Exit code propagation is tested through the bridge
SIGINT delivery to the spawned binary goes through the bridge's signal forwarding
Remove Strategy B (SDK client) — test the real binary only, not the SDK
No direct use of node:child_process.spawn from the test harness to run opencode
Tests skip with clear reason if opencode binary is not on PATH
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Rewritten: sandbox JS code calls child_process.spawn('opencode', ...) through the bridge. Removed Strategy B (SDK client). All 9 test scenarios preserved: boot, output (canary), text format, JSON format, env forwarding, file read, file write, SIGINT via bridge kill(), error handling. process.exit() must be at top-level await, not inside bridge callbacks (causes unhandled ProcessExitError). Bridge process.stdout.write strips trailing newlines — use newline-join in capture for NDJSON parsing.

userStories.title: Rewrite opencode-interactive.test.ts to use kernel.openShell() with opencode binary
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the OpenCode interactive tests to spawn the opencode binary through the sandbox's PTY so the tests validate the kernel's PTY management of external binaries.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace PtyHarness (host 'script -qefc' spawn) with kernel.openShell() or equivalent sandbox PTY mechanism
The opencode binary is launched from inside the sandbox, not directly from the test harness
Connect @xterm/headless to the sandbox's terminal output
All existing test scenarios preserved: TUI renders, input area works, text input, Ctrl+C interrupt
No direct use of node:child_process.spawn from the test harness to run opencode
Tests skip with clear reason if opencode binary is not on PATH or if kernel PTY is not ready
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Rewritten: replaced PtyHarness (host script -qefc) with kernel.openShell() + TerminalHarness. Uses overlay VFS (InMemoryFileSystem for populateBin, host FS fallback for module resolution). HostBinaryDriver (inline RuntimeDriver) handles 'opencode' and 'script' commands through the kernel. Probes detect: (1) openShell + node works, (2) child_process bridge spawns opencode through kernel, (3) streaming stdin from PTY. Currently skips on probe 3: NodeRuntimeDriver batches stdin for exec() instead of streaming — interactive PTY requires process.stdin events from PTY. All 5 test scenarios preserved and ready for when streaming stdin bridge is implemented.

userStories.title: Rewrite claude-headless.test.ts to spawn claude via sandbox child_process bridge
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Claude Code headless tests to spawn the claude binary from inside the secure-exec sandbox via the child_process.spawn bridge, so the tests validate the bridge handles Claude Code's complex subprocess lifecycle.
acceptanceCriteria: Test creates a NodeRuntime and runs JS code inside the sandbox that calls child_process.spawn('claude', ['-p', ...])
The sandbox's child_process bridge spawns the real claude binary on the host
Environment variables (ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) are forwarded through the bridge
All existing test scenarios preserved: boot, text output, JSON output, stream-json output, file read/write, bash tool, error handling
No direct use of node:child_process.spawn from the test harness to run claude
Tests skip with clear reason if claude binary is not on PATH
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Rewritten: sandbox JS code calls child_process.spawn('claude', ...) through the bridge. All 10 test scenarios preserved: boot, text output (canary), JSON output, stream-json NDJSON, file read (Read tool), file write (Write tool), bash tool, SIGINT via bridge kill(), bad API key exit code, good prompt exit 0. Claude natively supports ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL — no fetch interceptor needed. stream-json requires --verbose flag.

userStories.notes: Rewritten: replaced PtyHarness (host script -qefc) with kernel.openShell() + TerminalHarness. Uses overlay VFS (InMemoryFileSystem for populateBin, host FS fallback for module resolution). HostBinaryDriver (inline RuntimeDriver) handles 'claude' and 'script' commands through the kernel. Probes detect: (1) openShell + node works, (2) child_process bridge spawns claude through kernel, (3) streaming stdin from PTY. userStories.title: Update cli-tool-e2e spec to reflect actual tool capabilities
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the spec to accurately document which tools can run as JS inside the VM vs which must be spawned as binaries, so future iterations don't repeat the same mistakes.
acceptanceCriteria: Update docs-internal/specs/cli-tool-e2e.md to document: Pi is pure JS and runs inside the VM; Claude Code and OpenCode are native binaries that must be spawned via the child_process bridge
Remove or update the 'Binary-as-child-process mode' section to clarify this applies to Claude Code too, not just OpenCode
Document that OpenCode ships as a compiled Bun ELF binary with no JS source — it cannot be loaded in the VM
Document that Claude Code's SDK always spawns cli.js as a subprocess and has native .node dependencies
Remove the OpenCode SDK Strategy B from the spec (per user direction: test the real binary, not the SDK)
Update the 'Two sandbox strategies' section to accurately reflect: in-VM (Pi only) and bridge-spawn (Claude Code, OpenCode)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Updated: spec now documents Pi as the only in-VM tool; Claude Code and OpenCode both use bridge-spawn. Removed Strategy B (SDK). Documented Claude Code native binary reality (.node addons, sdk.mjs spawns cli.js). Updated gap analysis, risks, and success criteria.

userStories.title: Create S3 filesystem driver example with MinIO test setup
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an example showing how to implement a VirtualFileSystem backed by S3 so I can store sandbox files in object storage.
acceptanceCriteria: S3FileSystem class implements full VirtualFileSystem interface
Works with any S3-compatible store (AWS, MinIO, R2)
Docker Compose file for local MinIO test server
Example creates NodeRuntime with S3 filesystem, writes/reads files from sandbox, verifies via S3 API
README with instructions to start MinIO and run the test
Typecheck passes
Tests pass end-to-end against MinIO
userStories.notes: Completed. Example at examples/virtual-file-system-s3/. Uses @aws-sdk/client-s3. Tested e2e against MinIO Docker container. Symlinks/hard links throw ENOSYS; chmod/chown/utimes are no-ops.

userStories.title: Create SQLite filesystem driver example with sql.js
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an example showing how to implement a VirtualFileSystem backed by SQLite so I can store sandbox files in a database.
acceptanceCriteria: SQLiteFileSystem class implements full VirtualFileSystem interface
Uses sql.js (WASM) — no native compilation required
Supports symlinks, hard links, chmod, utimes, truncate
Supports snapshot/restore via export()/create(bytes)
Example creates NodeRuntime with SQLite filesystem, writes/reads files from sandbox, verifies via VFS API
README with instructions to run the test
Typecheck passes
Tests pass end-to-end
userStories.notes: Completed. Example at examples/virtual-file-system-sqlite/. Uses sql.js (WASM SQLite). All entries stored in single 'entries' table. Tested e2e — no external services needed.

userStories.title: Link filesystem driver examples in docs and describe use cases
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the filesystem driver examples linked in the custom virtual filesystem docs page with descriptions of use cases so I can discover them.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/features/virtual-filesystem.mdx links to both S3 and SQLite examples
Use case descriptions for each example
Additional use case ideas listed (Git-backed, encrypted, overlay, remote)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed. Added 'More examples' section with CardGroup linking to S3 and SQLite examples on GitHub, plus a list of other VFS use cases.

userStories.title: Add SSH key-based authentication e2e-docker fixture

userStories.title: Add SSH port forwarding / tunneling e2e-docker fixture
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an SSH fixture that tests TCP port forwarding through the sandbox so we validate that conn.forwardOut() works for database tunneling scenarios.
acceptanceCriteria: New fixture ssh2-tunnel in tests/e2e-docker/ that opens an SSH connection and uses forwardOut to tunnel to a service
Can tunnel to the existing Postgres or Redis container through the SSH container as a jump host

fixture.json expectation is 'pass'
Host and sandbox produce identical normalized output

Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed. Fixture tunnels through SSH to Redis using forwardOut(). Test infra discovers container internal IPs on the Docker bridge via docker inspect. Sends raw Redis PING through the tunnel, verifies +PONG response. Host and sandbox produce identical output.

userStories.title: Add SFTP directory operations e2e-docker fixture
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an SFTP fixture that tests directory operations (mkdir, rmdir, readdir) through the sandbox so we validate the full SFTP subsystem works.
acceptanceCriteria: New fixture ssh2-sftp-dirs in tests/e2e-docker/ that connects via SSH, opens SFTP, and tests directory ops
Fixture creates directory, lists it with readdir, creates a file inside, reads directory again, removes file, removes directory
fixture.json expectation is 'pass'
Host and sandbox produce identical normalized output
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed. Fixture ssh2-sftp-dirs connects via SSH, opens SFTP, creates directory, lists empty dir, writes file inside, lists dir with file, removes file, removes directory. Host and sandbox produce identical output.

userStories.title: Add SSH/SFTP error path e2e-docker fixtures
userStories.description: As a developer, I need fixtures that test SSH error paths (connection refused, auth failure) through the sandbox so we validate error reporting matches host behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: New fixture ssh2-auth-fail that connects with wrong password and verifies the error matches host behavior
New fixture ssh2-connect-refused that connects to a non-listening port and verifies the error matches host behavior
Both fixtures have fixture.json expectation 'pass' (they should produce the same error output on host and sandbox)
Host and sandbox produce identical normalized stdout/stderr/exit code
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed. ssh2-auth-fail connects with wrong password, verifies error level and message match host. ssh2-connect-refused connects to non-listening port 1, verifies ECONNREFUSED error message matches host. Both fixtures have expectation pass. Note: bridge _onError does not propagate err.code/err.errno (only message) — a future bridge improvement.

userStories.title: Add SFTP large file transfer and rename e2e-docker fixture
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an SFTP fixture that tests larger file transfers and rename operations through the sandbox so we validate TCP buffer management and stream backpressure.
acceptanceCriteria: New fixture ssh2-sftp-large in tests/e2e-docker/ that transfers a file of at least 1MB via SFTP through the sandbox
Fixture uses createWriteStream for upload and createReadStream for download (not just readFile)
Fixture tests sftp.rename() on the remote file
Verifies data integrity via hash comparison after round-trip
fixture.json expectation is 'pass'
Host and sandbox produce identical normalized output
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed. Fixture ssh2-sftp-large generates deterministic 1MB payload, uploads via createWriteStream, renames via sftp.rename(), downloads via createReadStream, verifies integrity via SHA-256 hash comparison. Host and sandbox produce identical output.

userStories.title: Expand pg-connect fixture with UPDATE, DELETE, and transactions
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Postgres fixture to test all CRUD operations and transactions so we validate the sandbox handles the full pg wire protocol.
acceptanceCriteria: Add UPDATE statement to pg-connect fixture (update the inserted row, verify the change with SELECT)
Add DELETE statement (delete the row, verify with SELECT returning 0 rows)
Add a transaction block: BEGIN, INSERT, ROLLBACK, verify the row was NOT inserted
Add a transaction block: BEGIN, INSERT, COMMIT, verify the row WAS inserted
fixture.json expectation remains 'pass'
Host and sandbox produce identical normalized output
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed. Expanded pg-connect fixture with UPDATE+verify, DELETE+verify, BEGIN/ROLLBACK (verify row not inserted), BEGIN/COMMIT (verify row inserted). All operations pass parity between host and sandbox.

userStories.title: Add pg-pool e2e-docker fixture for connection pooling

Fixture acquires a client from the pool, runs a query, releases it back
Fixture runs multiple concurrent queries via pool.query() shorthand
Fixture calls pool.end() for cleanup
fixture.json expectation is 'pass'
Host and sandbox produce identical normalized output
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed. Fixture pg-pool tests pool.connect()+client.query()+client.release(), pool.query() shorthand, concurrent queries via Promise.all, and pool.end(). Host and sandbox produce identical output.

userStories.title: Add pg-types e2e-docker fixture for data type coverage
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Postgres fixture that tests JSON, timestamps, bytea, arrays, and other data types so we validate the sandbox's type parser support.
acceptanceCriteria: New fixture pg-types in tests/e2e-docker/ that creates a table with columns: JSON, JSONB, TIMESTAMPTZ, BOOLEAN, BYTEA, INTEGER[], TEXT[], UUID, NUMERIC
Fixture inserts a row with values for each type, reads it back, verifies round-trip fidelity
Fixture verifies that pg type parsers return the correct JavaScript types (object for JSON, Date for timestamp, Buffer for bytea, Array for arrays)
fixture.json expectation is 'pass'
Host and sandbox produce identical normalized output
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed. Fixture pg-types creates table with JSON, JSONB, TIMESTAMPTZ, BOOLEAN, BYTEA, INTEGER[], TEXT[], UUID, NUMERIC columns. Inserts fixed test values, reads back, verifies JavaScript types (object for JSON, Date for timestamp, Buffer for bytea, Array for arrays). Host and sandbox produce identical output.

userStories.title: Add pg-errors e2e-docker fixture for error path coverage
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Postgres fixture that tests error handling so we validate the sandbox propagates Postgres errors correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: New fixture pg-errors in tests/e2e-docker/ that tests multiple error scenarios
Test 1: syntax error (bad SQL) — verify error message and code match host
Test 2: query nonexistent table — verify ENOENT-style error matches host
Test 3: unique constraint violation (INSERT duplicate) — verify error matches host
Test 4: connection to wrong port — verify connection error matches host
All errors are caught and their properties (message, code, detail) are printed as JSON for parity comparison
fixture.json expectation is 'pass'
Host and sandbox produce identical normalized output
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed. Fixture pg-errors tests 4 error scenarios: syntax error (bad SQL, code 42601), undefined table (code 42P01), unique constraint violation (code 23505), and connection refused (port 1). SQL errors serialize message/code/severity/constraint from pg protocol. Connection error serializes message only (socket-level code/errno not propagated through sandbox net bridge). Host and sandbox produce identical output.

userStories.title: Add pg-prepared e2e-docker fixture for prepared statements
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Postgres fixture that tests named prepared statements so we validate the sandbox handles the Parse/Bind/Execute wire protocol correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: New fixture pg-prepared in tests/e2e-docker/ that uses named prepared statements
Fixture uses client.query({ name: 'insert-row', text: 'INSERT ...', values: [...] }) syntax
Fixture reuses the same named statement multiple times (exercises the prepared statement cache)
Fixture verifies results match host behavior
fixture.json expectation is 'pass'
Host and sandbox produce identical normalized output
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed. Fixture pg-prepared tests named prepared statements with insert (reused 3x), select (reused 2x), update, and delete. Exercises Parse/Bind/Execute wire protocol and prepared statement cache. Host and sandbox produce identical output.

userStories.title: Fix pg auth method discrepancy between local and CI
userStories.description: As a developer, I need local and CI Postgres containers to use the same authentication method so we don't silently test different code paths.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove POSTGRES_HOST_AUTH_METHOD: trust from local Docker setup in e2e-docker.test.ts
Or add the same trust setting to CI — both environments must match
If removing trust: verify SCRAM-SHA-256 auth works through the sandbox locally
If keeping trust: document why and add a separate fixture that tests SCRAM auth explicitly
Tests pass both locally and in CI
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Fixed: Removed POSTGRES_HOST_AUTH_METHOD: trust from local Docker setup. Implemented subtle.deriveBits (PBKDF2) in both guest-side SandboxSubtle and host-side bridge dispatcher. Local and CI now both use scram-sha-256 authentication. All pg fixtures pass with SCRAM auth.

userStories.title: Add TLS database connection e2e-docker fixtures
userStories.description: As a developer, I need e2e-docker fixtures that test TLS-encrypted database connections so we validate the sandbox's HTTPS/TLS bridge handles SSL database connections correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: New fixture pg-ssl in tests/e2e-docker/ that connects to Postgres with ssl: { rejectUnauthorized: false }
Configure the Postgres container with SSL enabled (self-signed cert)
Fixture connects, runs a query, verifies the connection was encrypted (check pg_stat_ssl or similar)
fixture.json expectation is 'pass'
Host and sandbox produce identical normalized output
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed. Implemented tls.connect() bridge for TLS socket upgrade. Host wraps existing net.Socket with tls.TLSSocket, re-wires bridge callbacks for decrypted data. TLSSocket forwards end/close events to wrapped raw socket (pg relies on original socket's 'close' listener for shutdown). Custom postgres-ssl.Dockerfile with self-signed cert. Fixture connects with ssl:{rejectUnauthorized:false}, queries pg_stat_ssl to verify encryption, runs CRUD through TLS. Host and sandbox produce identical output.

userStories.title: Add HTTPS error handling e2e fixture for TLS edge cases
userStories.description: As a developer, I need tests that verify the sandbox correctly handles TLS error cases like expired certs, hostname mismatches, and self-signed certs with rejectUnauthorized:true.
acceptanceCriteria: New test cases in tests/runtime-driver/node/ that test HTTPS error scenarios through the sandbox
Test 1: fetch() to HTTPS server with expired cert — verify error matches host
Test 2: fetch() to HTTPS server with hostname mismatch — verify error matches host
Test 3: fetch() with rejectUnauthorized:true to self-signed cert — verify rejection matches host
All errors caught and compared for parity with host Node.js behavior
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Completed. Three TLS error scenarios tested with parity: expired cert (CERT_HAS_EXPIRED), hostname mismatch (ERR_TLS_CERT_ALTNAME_MISMATCH), self-signed cert with rejectUnauthorized:true (SELF_SIGNED_CERT_IN_CHAIN). Each test generates certs via openssl, starts HTTPS servers, and compares host vs sandbox error messages. TLS errors propagate correctly through the httpRequest bridge.

userStories.title: Verify Pi works via createAgentSession() SDK inside the sandbox
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to verify that Pi's programmatic SDK (createAgentSession) works end-to-end when loaded and executed inside the secure-exec sandbox VM.
acceptanceCriteria: Test creates a NodeRuntime with network bridge enabled
Pi's createAgentSession() from @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent is called inside the sandbox VM
Mock LLM server runs on host; sandbox reaches it via ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL through network bridge
Session boots, sends a prompt, receives a response, and completes without error
Pi's built-in tools (bash, read, write) work through the sandbox's child_process and fs bridges
Verify file write tool creates a file readable via the sandbox's fs bridge
Verify bash tool executes a command via the sandbox's child_process bridge
Skip with clear reason if @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent is not installed
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Fixed: (1) ESM dynamic import() transform in compileESMModule — isolated-vm V8 doesn't support native import() in ESM modules, now transformed to __dynamicImport() with module-scoped referrer for relative path resolution. (2) crypto.randomUUID overlay on crypto module bridge. (3) Headers.append() method. (4) FormData stub for SDK compatibility. (5) Response.body ReadableStream-like with getReader() for SSE streaming. (6) process.stdout/stderr.write callback support (Pi's runPrintMode flush). (7) Network adapter redirect for Anthropic API requests to mock server (sandbox fetch is non-writable). All 6 tests pass: boot, output canary, file read via tool, file write via tool, bash via tool, JSON mode.

userStories.title: Verify Pi headless binary mode inside the sandbox
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to verify that Pi's CLI binary (node dist/cli.js -p '...') works when spawned via the sandbox's child_process bridge in headless mode.
acceptanceCriteria: Test creates a NodeRuntime and runs JS that calls child_process.spawn('node', ['path/to/pi/dist/cli.js', '-p', '...'])
The child_process bridge spawns the real Pi CLI on the host
Mock LLM server runs on host; Pi reaches it via ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL env var forwarded through the bridge
Pi boots, processes the prompt, produces text output on stdout, and exits with code 0
Stdout from the binary flows back through the bridge correctly
Exit code propagation works through the bridge
Skip if @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent is not installed
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: This tests the child_process bridge path for Pi's CLI. Different from US-006 which runs Pi's JS directly in the VM. Here we verify the binary execution path works.

userStories.title: Verify Pi full TTY mode inside the sandbox
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to verify that Pi's interactive TUI works when launched through kernel.openShell() with a PTY.
acceptanceCriteria: Pi is launched inside the sandbox PTY via kernel.openShell()
Connect @xterm/headless to the sandbox's terminal output
Pi's TUI renders correctly — prompt area visible, input works
Text input appears in the prompt area
Submitting a prompt produces output from the mock LLM
Ctrl+C interrupts the current operation
Skip if @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent is not installed or kernel PTY is not ready
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: This is the full interactive verification. Depends on kernel.openShell() and isTTY bridge working. If the PTY infrastructure isn't ready, this should skip with a clear reason.

userStories.title: Verify Claude Code works via @anthropic-ai/claude-code SDK inside the sandbox
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to verify that the Claude Code SDK works when its ProcessTransport spawns cli.js through the sandbox's child_process bridge.
acceptanceCriteria: Test creates a NodeRuntime and runs JS that imports @anthropic-ai/claude-code SDK
SDK's ProcessTransport spawns claude cli.js via the sandbox's child_process bridge
Mock LLM server runs on host; Claude Code reaches it via ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL forwarded through the bridge
SDK sends a prompt and receives a text response
Verify the SDK can handle streaming responses through the bridge
Skip if @anthropic-ai/claude-code or claude binary is not available
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Claude Code SDK always spawns cli.js as a subprocess via ProcessTransport. The SDK itself runs as JS in the VM, but the underlying binary is spawned through the child_process bridge. This verifies the full SDK→bridge→binary pipeline.

userStories.title: Verify Claude Code headless binary mode inside the sandbox
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to verify that the claude binary works in headless -p mode when spawned via the sandbox's child_process bridge.
acceptanceCriteria: Test creates a NodeRuntime and runs JS that calls child_process.spawn('claude', ['-p', '...', '--output-format', 'text'])
The child_process bridge spawns the real claude binary on the host
Mock LLM server runs on host; Claude Code reaches it via ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL
Claude boots, processes the prompt, produces text output on stdout, exits with code 0
Test also verifies JSON output format (--output-format json)
Test also verifies stream-json output format (--output-format stream-json)
Exit code propagation works through the bridge
Skip if claude binary is not on PATH
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: This tests the raw binary spawn path without the SDK layer. Verifies stdio piping, env forwarding, and exit code propagation through the child_process bridge for Claude Code specifically.

userStories.title: Verify Claude Code full TTY mode inside the sandbox
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to verify that Claude Code's interactive TUI (Ink-based) works when launched through kernel.openShell() with a PTY.
acceptanceCriteria: Claude is launched inside the sandbox PTY via kernel.openShell()
Connect @xterm/headless to the sandbox's terminal output
Claude's TUI renders correctly — Ink UI visible, input area works
Text input appears in the prompt area
Submitting a prompt produces output from the mock LLM
Ctrl+C interrupts the current operation
/exit or Ctrl+D exits the session cleanly
Skip if claude binary is not on PATH or kernel PTY is not ready
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Claude Code uses Ink for its TUI rendering. This is the hardest verification — requires PTY, isTTY, signal delivery, and Ink's terminal management all working through the sandbox.

userStories.title: Verify OpenCode headless binary mode inside the sandbox
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to verify that the opencode binary works in headless 'run' mode when spawned via the sandbox's child_process bridge.
acceptanceCriteria: Test creates a NodeRuntime and runs JS that calls child_process.spawn('opencode', ['run', '...'])
The child_process bridge spawns the real opencode binary on the host
Mock LLM server runs on host; OpenCode reaches it via configured env vars
OpenCode boots, processes the prompt, produces output on stdout, exits cleanly
Stdout/stderr flow back through the bridge correctly
Exit code propagation works through the bridge
SIGINT delivery to the spawned binary works through the bridge
Skip if opencode binary is not on PATH
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: OpenCode is a compiled Bun binary (ELF) — no SDK, no JS source. The only way to run it is via child_process.spawn through the bridge. This is the headless verification.

userStories.title: Verify OpenCode full TTY mode inside the sandbox
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to verify that OpenCode's interactive TUI works when launched through kernel.openShell() with a PTY.
acceptanceCriteria: OpenCode is launched inside the sandbox PTY via kernel.openShell()
Connect @xterm/headless to the sandbox's terminal output
OpenCode's TUI renders correctly — UI visible, input area works
Text input appears in the input area
Submitting a prompt produces output from the mock LLM
Ctrl+C interrupts the current operation
Skip if opencode binary is not on PATH or kernel PTY is not ready
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: OpenCode is a native binary. This tests the full PTY path through kernel.openShell() for a non-Node binary.

userStories.title: Verify agent tool use round-trips through the sandbox for Pi
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to verify that Pi's tools (file read, file write, bash exec) work correctly through the sandbox bridges during a real multi-tool conversation.
acceptanceCriteria: Test runs Pi via createAgentSession() inside the sandbox with mock LLM configured to request tool calls
Mock LLM requests a file_write tool call — verify the file is created in the sandbox's VFS
Mock LLM requests a file_read tool call — verify the file content is returned correctly through the fs bridge
Mock LLM requests a bash tool call (e.g. 'echo hello') — verify stdout is captured through the child_process bridge
Mock LLM requests a bash tool call that fails (e.g. 'exit 1') — verify the error/exit code propagates back
Tool results are sent back to the mock LLM correctly for the next turn
Skip if @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent is not installed
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: This specifically tests tool execution round-trips, not just prompt→response. The mock LLM must be configured to return tool_use responses that trigger Pi's built-in tools.

userStories.title: Verify agent tool use round-trips through the sandbox for Claude Code
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to verify that Claude Code's tools (file read, file write, bash exec) work correctly through the sandbox's child_process bridge during a real conversation.
acceptanceCriteria: Test spawns claude via child_process bridge in headless -p mode with mock LLM
Mock LLM requests a write_to_file tool call — verify the file is created on the host (via the bridge)
Mock LLM requests a read_file tool call — verify the file content is returned correctly
Mock LLM requests a bash tool call — verify the command executes and stdout is captured
Mock LLM requests a bash tool call that fails — verify the error propagates back correctly
Claude completes the conversation and exits cleanly after tool use
Skip if claude binary is not on PATH
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Claude Code's tools execute as subprocesses of the claude process, which is itself spawned through the child_process bridge. This is a nested process scenario. The mock LLM must return tool_use blocks.

userStories.title: Verify multi-turn agentic loop works through the sandbox for Pi
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to verify that a multi-turn conversation with Pi works inside the sandbox — the agent reads a file, modifies it, runs a test, and fixes an error across multiple turns.
acceptanceCriteria: Test runs Pi via createAgentSession() inside the sandbox with mock LLM
Set up a simple JS project in the sandbox VFS (package.json + index.js + index.test.js with a failing test)
Turn 1: Mock LLM tells Pi to read the test file — Pi reads it via file_read tool through fs bridge
Turn 2: Mock LLM tells Pi to read the source file — Pi reads it
Turn 3: Mock LLM tells Pi to write a fixed version — Pi writes via file_write tool through fs bridge
Turn 4: Mock LLM tells Pi to run the test — Pi runs 'node index.test.js' via bash tool through child_process bridge
Verify the test output shows success
All 4 turns complete without error, state persists across turns within the same session
Skip if @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent is not installed
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: This tests the full agentic loop: read → understand → modify → verify. Each turn uses different bridges (fs, child_process) and state must persist across turns. The mock LLM drives the conversation with pre-scripted responses.

userStories.title: Verify npm install works inside the sandbox via child_process bridge
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to verify that npm install works when executed through the sandbox's child_process bridge, since coding agents frequently install packages.
acceptanceCriteria: Test creates a NodeRuntime with child_process and network bridges enabled
Write a package.json with a small dependency (e.g. 'is-odd') to the sandbox VFS
Execute 'npm install' via child_process.spawn through the bridge
npm downloads the package from the real npm registry through the network bridge
node_modules directory is created with the installed package
Verify the installed package can be require()'d in a subsequent exec() call
Exit code is 0
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: npm install exercises child_process bridge (spawns npm), network bridge (downloads from registry), and filesystem (writes node_modules). This is one of the most common agent operations and a major integration test.

userStories.title: Verify npx works inside the sandbox via child_process bridge
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to verify that npx can download and execute a package through the sandbox, since agents use npx for one-off tool execution.
acceptanceCriteria: Test creates a NodeRuntime with child_process and network bridges enabled
Execute 'npx cowsay hello' or similar lightweight npx command via child_process.spawn through the bridge
npx downloads the package, executes it, and produces output on stdout
Output flows back through the child_process bridge correctly
Exit code is 0
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: npx is a common agent operation. It combines package download (network), temp installation (fs), and execution (child_process) in one command.

userStories.title: Verify dev server lifecycle inside the sandbox (start, verify, kill)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to verify that a long-running dev server can be started, verified, and killed through the sandbox, since agents frequently start servers to test their work.
acceptanceCriteria: Test creates a NodeRuntime with child_process and network bridges enabled
Write a simple HTTP server script to the sandbox VFS (e.g. http.createServer listening on a random port)
Spawn the server via child_process.spawn through the bridge
Verify the server is listening by making an HTTP request through the network bridge
The HTTP response comes back correctly through the network bridge
Send SIGTERM to the server process via the child_process bridge's kill()
Verify the process exits within 5 seconds
No zombie processes remain after cleanup
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: This tests the full dev server lifecycle that agents commonly use: start a server, verify it works, then tear it down. Exercises long-running process management, port binding through the net bridge, and signal delivery.

userStories.title: Update cli-tool-e2e spec to reflect verification levels and agentic workflow tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the spec to document the three verification levels (SDK, headless binary, full TTY), tool use verification, and agentic workflow tests.
acceptanceCriteria: Update docs-internal/specs/cli-tool-e2e.md to document the three verification levels: SDK, headless binary, full TTY
Document: Pi supports all 3 levels (SDK via createAgentSession, headless via cli.js, TTY)
Document: Claude Code supports all 3 levels (SDK via @anthropic-ai/claude-code, headless via claude -p, TTY)
Document: OpenCode supports 2 levels (headless binary, TTY) — no SDK available, compiled Bun ELF
Document the tool use verification approach (mock LLM with tool_use responses)
Document the agentic workflow tests (multi-turn, npm install, dev server lifecycle)
Remove the OpenCode SDK Strategy B from the spec
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The spec needs to reflect the full verification matrix and document the agentic workflow test approach.
